message:
feat(mvcc): replace GC scheduler with batch-epoch EBR reclamation

Overhaul the MVCC version reclamation system from a scheduler-driven
garbage collector to a fine-grained epoch-based reclamation (EBR)
scheme that tracks per-batch retirement epochs against active reader
pins, enabling partial draining and bounded memory usage under
concurrent load.

EBR epoch tracking (ebr.rs):
- VersionGuardRegistry gains global_epoch counter and per-guard
  pinned_epoch tracking (set from current_epoch at registration time)
- New methods: current_epoch(), advance_epoch(), advance_epoch_to()
  (CAS loop), min_pinned_epoch() for reclaimability decisions
- EbrRetireQueue rewritten from flat Vec<VersionIdx> to
  Vec<RetiredBatch> where each batch carries its retire_epoch
- drain_if_safe now drains batch-by-batch using epoch comparison
  against min_pinned_epoch, enabling partial reclamation of only
  those batches whose retire epoch is strictly below all active guards

VersionStore (invariants.rs):
- Remove gc_epoch field; epoch now lives in guard_registry
- gc_tick() delegates to guard_registry.advance_epoch()
- try_recycle_retired_slots() removes hardcoded MIN_EPOCH_GAP constant
  and active-guard-count heuristic; now uses advance_epoch_to() +
  min_pinned_epoch() for precise reclaimability via batch-aware drain
- New arena introspection: arena_high_water(), arena_free_count()

Lifecycle integration (lifecycle.rs):
- advance_epoch() called after complete_version_maintenance()
- try_recycle_retired_slots() called after releasing version guard

Tests:
- Multi-epoch retirement test verifying partial batch drain
- test_ebr_8t_bounded_memory: 8-thread concurrent publish/prune/
  advance verifying bounded live slot count under contention

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -78,7 +78,7 @@ fsqlite-wal  fsqlite-btree
 
 - `VersionGuardRegistry` / `VersionGuard` -- Epoch-based reader registration for safe old-version reclamation.
 - `QsbrRegistry` / `QsbrHandle` -- Quiescent-state-based reclamation (QSBR) for lock-free data structures.
-- `GcScheduler` / `gc_tick` / `prune_page_chain` -- Background garbage collection of unreachable page versions.
+- `EbrRetireQueue` / `VersionStore` / `prune_page_chain` -- Commit-time retirement and batch recycling of unreachable page versions once pinned readers advance.
 - `RcuCell` / `RcuPair` / `RcuTriple` -- Read-copy-update primitives.
 
 ### Concurrency Primitives
